title: DORA ICT Third-Party Risk Register — Inherent vs Residual Risk Score Reconciliation

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run, then normalised in Phase A (clause 'unspecified' → 'Art.19'). Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

spl:
index=risk sourcetype="dora:tprr" earliest=-1d
| where inherent>0 AND residual=inherent AND (isnull(control_effectiveness) OR control_effectiveness="unknown")
| eval last=strptime(last_review,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where last<relative_time(now(),"-180d@d")
| table provider_id, inherent, residual, control_effectiveness, last_review
value: Risk registers should show mitigation effect. Detecting rows where residual risk equals inherent without documented controls, or where math is inconsistent, protects the integrity of supervisory reporting derived from the register.
implementation: (1) Define scoring scale 1–25; (2) require control linkage from GRC; (3) quarterly risk committee review of hits; (4) exclude not-yet-assessed new vendors for 90 days via `onboarding_flag`; (5) export for internal audit.
visualization: Table (anomalies), Scatter (inherent vs residual), Single value (count).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
